conclusion: 5-cohort contract improved intraday family from 5/9 to 6/9. Topk perturbation bottleneck materially relieved. Absolute return/IR and cost_stress still failed. Intraday family under 5-cohort is still not viable under prereg success rules.

runs.note: best cost-stress profile among intraday candidates

diagnostic_takeaway: Under the 5-cohort contract, the intraday family became materially closer to viable than under the 26-cohort contract (5/9 -> 6/9, topk perturbation fixed), but still failed prereg success rules due to insufficient absolute return/IR and negative cost-stress performance. The contract change solved the topk perturbation bottleneck (was a contract problem). The remaining bottlenecks (absolute return, cost_stress) are signal-level problems, not contract problems.
note_on_turnover_gate_reporting: c3_5cohort turnover_delta=0.0030, PASS (<=0.04). An earlier inline report script had a inequality-direction bug that marked it as FAIL. The actual data is correct.
next_direction_candidate: Signal-layer improvement: intraday_trend_bias alone (c1) is the strongest single intraday signal, consistently beating v18 reference. Its remaining gap is absolute return and cost_stress. These require a different contract or a fundamentally different signal approach, not further intraday family expansion.
